Records.description: The integrals in this paper are given as analytic functions in $d$ dimensions, so that all orders in $\varepsilon$ are covered. The common prefactor to all integrals is $(4\pi)(4 - d)/2 (\Gamma(3 - d/2) \Gamma^2(d/2 - 1))/\Gamma(d - 3)$. Be aware that all these integrals have at least one off-shell leg.

Results.resultTeXPreview: \frac{3 M^{-2}}{4 \varepsilon^3 m^2}+\frac{M^{-4} \left(-m^2 \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\right)+m^2 \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)-3 M^2 \log (m^2)-M^2 \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)\right)}{2 \varepsilon^2 m^2}+\frac{M^{-4} \left(-6 m^2 H\left(0,1-m^2 M^{-2}\,;1\right)-6 m^2 H\left(0,1-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\,;1\right)-6 m^2 H\left(\frac{m^2}{M^2+m^2},1-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\,;1\right)-6 m^2 H\left(1-\frac{M^2}{m^2},\frac{m^2}{M^2+m^2}\,;1\right)+6 M^2 H\left(0,1-m^2 M^{-2}\,;1\right)+6 M^2 H\left(0,\frac{m^2}{M^2+m^2}\,;1\right)+18 M^2 H\left(0,1-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\,;1\right)+6 M^2 H\left(\frac{m^2}{M^2+m^2},1-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\,;1\right)+18 M^2 \log ^2(m^2)-6 m^2 \log ^2\left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\right)+12 m^2 \log ^2\left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)-3 M^2 \log ^2\left(m^2 M^{-2}\right)-3 M^2 \log ^2\left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\right)+3 M^2 \log ^2\left(\frac{M^2}{m^2}\right)-18 M^2 \log ^2\left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)+12 m^2 \log (m^2) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\right)-12 m^2 \log (m^2) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)+12 M^2 \log (m^2) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)-18 m^2 \log \left(-m^2 M^{-2}\right) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2}\right)+18 m^2 \log \left(-m^2 M^{-2}\right) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)+6 m^2 \log \left(\frac{m^2}{m^2-M^2}\right) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)+6 M^2 \log \left(-m^2 M^{-2}\right) \log \left(m^2 M^{-2}\right)+6 M^2 \log \left(-m^2 M^{-2}\right) \log \left(\frac{M^2}{m^2}\right)-18 M^2 \log \left(-m^2 M^{-2}\right) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)-6 M^2 \log \left(\frac{m^2}{m^2-M^2}\right) \log \left(-\frac{M^2}{m^2-M^2}\right)-3 \zeta(2) M^2+4 \pi ^2 M^2-\pi ^2 m^2\right)}{12 \varepsilon m^2}+(\text{more terms})+O\left(\varepsilon^1\right)
